_banner: FROZEN SNAPSHOT 2026-07 — harness design ADRs 0002-0034 vendored once for agent use. Project CI never reads the harness repo. H- prefix = harness namespace.
_collision_note: Harness numbering collision with project: H-0055=project-0056 (server-module-modularization), H-0056=project-0057 (content-directory-glob-loading), H-0057=project-0080 (generated-knowledge-bundle). See docs/adr/README.md for full explanation.

entries.title: Server platform & netcode
entries.decision: SpacetimeDB as the authoritative server platform with generated TypeScript client bindings and intent-only reducer calls.

entries.title: Shared rule core & client prediction
entries.decision: Shared Rust game-core crate holds all game rules; client-wasm runs the same deterministic logic for client-side prediction.

entries.title: Client rendering stack
entries.decision: PixiJS canvas renderer with a read-only SDK store, pure view functions, and no UI framework.

entries.title: Single cohesive repo with a cargo workspace
entries.decision: Cargo workspace in one repo holds server-module, game-core, client-wasm, and client to eliminate cross-repo drift.

entries.title: Schema evolution & content-sync
entries.decision: Additive-only schema migrations; content sync via versioned RON files with build-time validation and a CONTENT_VERSION guard.

entries.title: Spatial / zoned subscriptions & per-zone tick
entries.decision: Per-zone spatial subscriptions with a bounded intent queue and a scheduled per-zone movement tick reducer.

entries.title: Map / content authoring pipeline
entries.decision: Zone maps as tiled RON files; monsters, items, and dialogue as glob-loaded content-registry directories.

entries.title: CI completeness & merge gates
entries.decision: CI gates: lint, typecheck, test, eval, security, mutation, and coverage; every gate has a proof-of-teeth fixture.

entries.title: Falsifiable mechanical gates (proof-of-teeth)
entries.decision: Every CI gate must have a fixture that fails the gate when the guarded invariant is violated.

entries.title: Server-paced movement via a bounded queue + scheduled per-zone tick
entries.decision: Server resolves movement by draining a bounded per-zone intent queue in a scheduled reducer tick.

entries.title: Client prediction & reconciliation protocol
entries.decision: Client applies movement predictions locally and reconciles against server-authoritative state on each tick receipt.

entries.title: Netcode smoothness & feel
entries.decision: Netcode targets ≤150ms perceived latency with EWMA interpolation, snap-on-teleport, and a sim-harness convergence gate.

entries.title: Client app architecture (read-only store, one-way flow, no UI framework)
entries.decision: Read-only SDK store feeds pure view functions that produce canvas draw calls; no mutable client state or UI framework.

entries.title: Owner-scoped row privacy via RLS (the third visibility mode)
entries.decision: Must-never-leak data lives in private tables; owner-scoped rows use RLS; public tables are safe to expose unfiltered.

entries.title: Monster individuality & progression model (IV / EV / nature)
entries.decision: Monsters have birth IVs, training EVs, and a nature modifier; all three are private and never reach non-owner clients.

entries.title: Battle data model & server-resolved turn model (PvP-ready)
entries.decision: Server resolves every battle turn with game-core and writes results to battle/battle_state tables; PvP-ready from day one.

entries.title: Inventory & item model (one-stack, saturating helpers)
entries.decision: One inventory stack per item per player; quantities are capped and quantity helpers saturate rather than overflow.

entries.title: Evolution & fusion model (individuality-preserving + content integrity)
entries.decision: Evolution is a pure content-driven transform; fusion preserves individuality stats; both are validated at content load time.

entries.title: Zone transitions (warps) & subscription switching
entries.decision: Zone transitions use a server-authoritative warp table; the client switches subscriptions on warp receipt.

entries.title: Dialogue & quest system (data-driven trees + flag-based state)
entries.decision: Dialogue trees are data-driven RON; quest state is server-authoritative boolean flags in a private conversation table.

entries.title: Currency & shop economy
entries.decision: A single private player_wallet table with explicit grant/spend reducers; shop prices and sellback are content-defined.

entries.title: Additive battle-depth framework (status / abilities / weather)
entries.decision: Status effects, passive abilities, and weather are additive layers wired into resolve_full_turn as discrete pipeline phases.

entries.title: Cross-player escrow & dual-consent transactions
entries.decision: Cross-player trades use an escrow table with dual-consent reducers; no item moves until both parties commit.

entries.title: PvP orchestration (handshake, secret both-submit, liveness)
entries.decision: PvP match handshake uses secret-both-submit intent; a scheduled reducer enforces liveness with a timeout forfeit.

entries.title: Persistent ranked profile (Elo)
entries.decision: Persistent Elo ranked profile stored per-player; rating adjustments happen at match end via a post-match reducer.

entries.title: Co-op raid model (additive resolve_coop_turn)
entries.decision: Co-op raids use an additive resolve_coop_turn that merges player intents in a single server-resolved turn.

entries.title: Social subsystem (chat, guilds, moderation) — untrusted-content posture
entries.decision: Chat is untrusted content sanitized at read time; guilds are flat owner-member tables with explicit moderation reducers.

entries.title: Observability & performance strategy
entries.decision: Observability via SpacetimeDB metrics and client performance marks; latency budgets enforced in CI benchmarks.

entries.title: Authentication & account model
entries.decision: SpacetimeDB identity is the sole auth primitive; no separate auth service; accounts are identity-keyed tables.

entries.title: Privacy & data lifecycle (deletion, export, retention)
entries.decision: Player data deleted within 30 days of request; export on request; retention periods defined per-table with a scheduled purge.

entries.title: Accessibility strategy
entries.decision: Keyboard-navigable UI, contrast ≥4.5:1, and screen-reader ARIA labels on all interactive elements.

entries.title: Internationalization (i18n) strategy
entries.decision: i18n via a message-key map; locale bundles are data files, not code; the default locale is en-US.

entries.title: Security audit & threat model as a launch gate
entries.decision: A full security audit gate (M25) signs off the threat model and Semgrep clean before launch.

entries.title: Server-module internal module boundary (modularization for fan-out)
entries.decision: Server-module source is split into domain submodules registered via mod.rs declarations; cross-module imports are disallowed.

entries.title: Content as glob-loaded directories (build.rs)
entries.decision: Content registries are directories glob-loaded at compile time by build.rs; the type_chart stays a single-file exception.

entries.title: Generated knowledge bundle — OKF-conformant schema projection for agents
entries.decision: Server schema is projected as a generated OKF-conformant knowledge bundle; drift-gated by just knowledge-check in CI.
